dataset: TCGA-KIRC metastasis M1 vs M0 (n=505, 45 genes)

verdict.reason: The gate's PASS is defensible but not overwhelming. perm_p=0.0 and decoy_p=0.0 (with decoy_q95=0.566 well below law_auc=0.7256) are strong null rejections, and delta_baseline=0.070 clears the 0.05 margin. However, ci_width=0.1166 exceeds the 0.10 preferred band and only passes via the ci_lower=0.6646>0.6 escape clause, indicating borderline bootstrap stability given only 79 M1 cases. More concerning, confound_auc=0.5 implies the covariate-only model was essentially null/trivial, so delta_confound=0.226 is not a meaningful test of confounding — the gate effectively skipped this check. Mechanistically the sign is plausible (TOP2A up with proliferative/metastatic KIRC; EPAS1/HIF2A down-weighted is biologically interesting since HIF2A is a VHL-loss driver but may anti-correlate with late metastatic dedifferentiation), so the law is not absurd, but class imbalance (79/505) and the empty confound model are the likely failure modes for generalization.
verdict.additional_test: Re-run the confound-adjusted AUC using real clinical covariates (tumor stage, grade, age, sex) as the nuisance model to verify delta_confound remains >0.03 once a non-trivial baseline is in place.
